2606.11430.title: Towards a Bridge Layer Between Bibliographic and Formalized Mathematical Knowledge
2606.11430.paper_summary: Mathematical knowledge is split between bibliographic databases (e.g., MathSciNet, zbMATH Open) and formal proof libraries (e.g., Lean mathlib), preventing unified access between published results and their formalizations. We propose a relational bridge-database that aligns publication metadata with formal artifacts, providing an interoperability layer between mathematical literature and machine-verifiable proofs. We introduce a paper-level formalization score that measures how much of a publication is covered in formal systems. As a feasibility study, we show how such scores can be estimated via cross-document alignment between informal texts and Lean formalizations, enabling large-scale analysis of formalization coverage. This framework is a first step toward integrating bibliographic and formal mathematical ecosystems into scalable, machine-actionable knowledge graphs linking publications to formal proof objects.
2606.11430.paper_summary_zh: 數學知識分散在文獻資料庫（例如，MathSciNet、zbMATH Open）和正式證明庫（例如，Lean mathlib）之間，這阻礙了已發表結果與其形式化之間的統一訪問。我們提出了一個關聯橋接資料庫，將出版元數據與正式文物對齊，提供數學文獻與機器可驗證證明之間的互操作性層。我們引入了一個論文級別的形式化分數，衡量一篇出版物在正式系統中被覆蓋的程度。作為可行性研究，我們展示了如何通過非正式文本與Lean形式化之間的跨文檔對齊來估計這些分數，從而實現對形式化覆蓋的規模化分析。這一框架是將文獻和正式數學生態系統整合成可擴展的、機器可操作的知識圖譜，將出版物與正式證明對象連結的第一步。
2606.11430.author: A. Mayeux et.al.
